immutable-by-default.notes: All interfaces use readonly. Uses .map/.reduce, no mutation of cart or items. Good.

no-magic-numbers.notes: All numeric literals extracted into named config constants (WEIGHT_TIERS, COUNTRY_MULTIPLIERS, MAX_ITEM_WEIGHT_KG). Acceptable.

strict-typing.notes: No 'any' — good. BUT CartItem has '[key: string]: unknown' index signature which defeats type safety (anything can be accessed). 'country: string' is loose — should be a union type or branded type of supported countries (e.g. keyof typeof COUNTRY_MULTIPLIERS). The index signature is especially bad — it makes the type effectively untyped.

externalize-config.notes: All business params (tiers, multipliers, coupons, max weight) externalized to top-level constants. Changeable without editing function bodies.

bound-every-input.notes: weight <= 0 throws (GOOD). weight > MAX throws (GOOD). Unknown country throws (GOOD). Empty cart throws (GOOD). BUT: invalid coupon codes are silently ignored — isFreeShippingCoupon returns false for unknown coupons like 'BOGUS50' instead of throwing. The criteria explicitly requires: 'Invalid coupon -> throw/error (NOT silently return false)'. A coupon that exists but is not recognized should be rejected, not silently treated as no coupon.

deductions: Invalid coupon codes silently ignored — 'BOGUS50' is treated as no coupon instead of throwing an error
[key: string]: unknown index signature on CartItem undermines strict typing
'country: string' should be derived from COUNTRY_MULTIPLIERS keys for type safety

Section divider comments ('// --- Types ---', '// --- Domain Logic ---') violate the skill's own rule: 'Never write trivial comments — section dividers for type/const/import grouping — language keywords are the guide'
